implementationNote: Implementation scope confirmed with Talent Grid during product demo
pricingJustification: Partner pricing via Talent Grid · volume discounts available · localized implementation & first-line support
context: Gaming & betting company in Central Africa. ~180 employees; initial rollout with 150 seats. Existing HR platform handles performance but not trainings. Mandatory compliance trainings (AML, responsible gaming, gambling) via PDFs and external partner.
priorities: Link performance gaps to trainings, compliance training tracking, training budget visibility, integrate alongside existing HR stack (not rip-and-replace)
mobileStory: Employees complete trainings on mobile — new Factorial capability demonstrated on call
operationsStory: External and internal trainings tracked in one LMS; virtual sessions with external links; training groups and session costs
documentsStory: AI creates courses from PDF/Word uploads; quizzes and video content from existing materials
payrollStory: Talent Grid (Angela Wekesa, Evans Munga) — pricing, implementation, and first-line support
implementationStory: Localized account management via Talent Grid to reduce turnaround time on support requests
nextSteps: Sandbox access (Victor) → commercial alignment with dual pricing options → go-live 150 seats in 1–2 months
storySubline: 150 employees · gaps mapped after reviews · no training link today · integrate alongside your current HR stack
journeyTitle: The HR year at Choplife Gaming
journeySubline: Follow the performance cycle — from reviews and talent mapping to compliance training and budget control
journeyStepsSummary: Reviews close with gaps → structured workflows → 9-box talent view → performance drives learning → AI training + budgets

stakeholders.partner: Talent Grid — Angela Wekesa, Evans Munga
stakeholders.factorial: Victor Gutierrez — Partnership Account Manager, ROW
